title: Biphasic electrode suspension for a semi-solid flow cell
abstract: A biphasic electrode suspension for a semi-solid flow cell comprises a polar solvent, a nonionic dispersant, a first plurality of particles having repulsive interactions and comprising an electrochemically active material, and a second plurality of particles having attractive interactions and comprising an electronically conductive material. The nonionic dispersant is present in an amount sufficient to sterically stabilize the first plurality of particles, but insufficient to sterically stabilize the second plurality of particles. The second plurality of particles forms a percolating conductive network through the first plurality of particles.
claims: Claims 1. A biphasic electrode suspension for a semi-solid flow cell, the biphasic electrode suspension comprising: a polar solvent; a nonionic dispersant; a first plurality of particles having repulsive interactions and comprising an electrochemically active material; and a second plurality of particles having attractive interactions and comprising an electronically conductive material; wherein the nonionic dispersant is present in an amount sufficient to sterically stabilize the first plurality of particles, but insufficient to sterically stabilize the second plurality of particles, the second plurality of particles forming a percolating conductive network through the first plurality of particles. 2. The biphasic electrode suspension of claim 1 wherein the polar solvent comprises a nonaqueous polar solvent. 3. The biphasic electrode suspension of claim 1 or 2, wherein the polar solvent comprises a salt present at an ionic strength of up to about 1 M. 4. The biphasic electrode suspension of claim 3, wherein the salt comprises a lithium salt. 5. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the nonionic dispersant is selected from the group consisting of: polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), polyvinyl pyridine, octylphenol ethoxylate, a polymer based on an acrylic, ester, ether, vinyl acetate, vinyl ester, carbonate, or ketone, and a block copolymer with a combination of hydrophobic and hydrophilic groups. 6. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the amount of the nonionic dispersant is at least about 0.1 wt.%. 7. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the electrochemically active material is selected from the group consisting of: Li 4 Ti 5 O 12 (LTO), LiNio.5Mn 1.5O4 (LNMO), LiCoO 2 , LiFePO 4 (LFP), LiTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 , V 2 O 5 , LiV 3 O 8 , MnO 2 , Sn-based oxides and composite alloys. 8. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the electronically conductive material comprises carbon. 9. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the electrochemically active material is present at concentration of at least about 20 vol.%. 10. The biphasic electrode suspension of claim any one of claims 1- 9, wherein the conductive material is present at a concentration of at least about 0.1 vol.%. 11. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the first plurality of particles comprises a mean particle size of from about 0.1 to about 10 microns. 12. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-11 , wherein the first plurality of particles comprises a specific surface area of from about 1 m 2 /g to about 100 m 2 /g. 13. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the second plurality of particles comprises a mean particle size of from about 1 nm to about 1000 nm. 14. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the second plurality of particles comprises a specific surface area of from about 100 m 2 /g to about 2000 m 2 /g. 15. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-14 comprising an electronic conductivity of at least about 1 mS/cm. 16. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-15 comprising an apparent viscosity of no greater than about 10 4 Pa s at a shear rate of 10 "1 s "1 . 17. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-16 comprising a shear yield stress of no greater than about 700 Pa. 18. The biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the shear elastic storage modulus (G') is no greater than about 6 x 10 4 Pa. 19. The biphasic electrode suspension of claim 18, wherein the shear elastic storage modulus (G') is no greater than about 1 x 10 4 Pa. 20. An electrode for a semi-solid flow cell comprising the biphasic electrode suspension of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the electrode comprises a thickness of at least about 100 microns. 21. The electrode of claim 20 comprising a charge capacity of at least about 120 mAh/g at a rate of C/8 and a Coulombic efficiency of at least about 99%. 22. A method of making a biphasic electrode suspension for a semisolid flow cell, the method comprising: mixing together a polar solvent, a salt, a first plurality of particles comprising an electrochemically active material, and a nonionic dispersant to form a first suspension; and mixing into the first suspension a second plurality of particles comprising an electronically conductive material, thereby forming a biphasic electrode suspension. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the polar solvent, the first plurality of particles and the non-ionic dispersant are mixed together before adding the salt. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the polar solvent, the nonionic dispersant and the salt are mixed together before adding the first plurality of particles. 25. The method of any one of claims 22-24, wherein the mixing is carried out in a controlled environment comprising a moisture and oxygen content of less than 0.5 ppm. 26. The method of any one of claims 22-25, wherein the salt is present at an ionic strength of up to about 1 M. 27. The method of any one of claims 22-26, wherein the nonionic dispersant is selected from the group consisting of: polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), polyvinyl pyridine, octylphenol ethoxylate, a polymer based on an acrylic, ester, ether, vinyl acetate, vinyl ester, carbonate, or ketone, and a block copolymer with a combination of hydrophobic and hydrophilic groups. 28. The method of any one of claims 22-27, wherein the amount of the nonionic dispersant is at least about 0.1 wt.%. 29. The method of any one of claims 22-28, wherein the first plurality of particles comprises a mean particle size of from about 0.1 to about 10 microns. 30. The method of any one of claims 22-29, wherein the first plurality of particles comprises a specific surface area of from about 1 m 2 /g to about 100 m 2 /g. 31. The method of any one of claims 22-30, wherein the second plurality of particles comprises a mean particle size of from about 1 nm to about 1000 nm. 32. The method of any one of claims 22-31 , wherein the second plurality of particles comprises a specific surface area of from about 100 m 2 /g to about 2000 m 2 /g. 33. The method of any one of claims 22-32, wherein the electrochemically active material is present at concentration of at least about 20 vol.%. 34. The method of any one of claims 22-33, wherein the conductive material is present at a concentration of at least about 0.1 vol.% 35. The method of any one of claims 22-34, wherein the electrochemically active material is selected from the group consisting of: Li 4 Ti 5 Oi2 (LTO), LiNio.5Mn1.5O4 (LNMO), LiCoO 2 , LiFePO 4 (LFP), LiTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 , V2O5, L1V3O8, MnO 2 , Sn-based oxides and composite alloys. 36. The method of any one of claims 22-35, wherein the electronically conductive material comprises carbon. 37. The method of any one of claims 22-36, wherein the polar solvent comprises one or more of the following: propylene carbonate (PC), ethylene carbonate (EC), a linear ester or carbonate, a fluorinated ester, a fluorinated carbonate, a fluorinated ether, a cyclic carbonate, a sulfone, a sulfonamide, tetrahydrofuran (THF), 2-methyltetrahydrofuran (2-Me-THF), polymethoxy ether, dimethoxy propane, diethyl ether, diethyoxyethane (DEE), dimethoxyethane (DME), dimethyl carbonate (DMC), diethyl carbonate (DEC), ethylmethyl carbonate (EMC), propylmethyl carbonate (PMC)), chloroethylene carbonate (CIEC), fluoroethylene carbonate (FEC), trifluoropropylene carbonate (TFPC), Î³-butyrolactone (yBL), glycol borate ester (BEG), ethyl propionate (EP), ethyl butyrate (EB), and water.
